title: Supabase migration: add quiet_hours column to user_preferences
spec:
Create a Supabase migration that adds the quiet_hours JSONB column to the user_preferences table (creating the table if it does not exist).

If the table does not exist, create it:
```sql
CREATE TABLE user_preferences (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  quiet_hours jsonb DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
ALTER TABLE user_preferences ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "users manage own prefs" ON user_preferences
  FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

If the table already exists:
```sql
ALTER TABLE user_preferences
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS quiet_hours jsonb DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb;
```

Place the migration in supabase/migrations/ with an appropriate timestamp prefix. Verify RLS is enabled and the per-user policy covers SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE. The column default must be '[]'::jsonb (not null).
acceptance_tests:
Given a new user with no preferences row
When their row is created
Then user_preferences.quiet_hours defaults to '[]'::jsonb (AT-1)

Given two different users with separate user_preferences rows
When user A attempts to SELECT or UPDATE user B's row
Then the query returns no rows and no error (RLS enforced) (AT-9)

title: iOS: implement QuietHoursService (load/save/isQuietNow)
spec:
Create QuietHoursService.swift in the zazigv3 iOS app. The service must conform exactly to the spec:

```swift
struct QuietHoursEntry: Codable, Identifiable {
    var id = UUID()
    var day: String   // "mon".."sun"
    var start: String // "HH:MM" 24h
    var end: String   // "HH:MM" 24h
}

class QuietHoursService: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = QuietHoursService()
    @Published var entries: [QuietHoursEntry] = []

    func load() async  // fetch quiet_hours from user_preferences via Supabase client
    func save() async  // upsert entries array to user_preferences.quiet_hours
    func isQuietNow() -> Bool
}
```

isQuietNow() algorithm:
1. Get current weekday as lowercased 3-letter string ("mon"–"sun") using Calendar.current.
2. Get current time as "HH:MM" string.
3. Filter entries matching today's day code.
4. For each matching entry:
   - If start <= end: return true if start <= now <= end.
   - If start > end (midnight-spanning): return true if now >= start OR now <= end.
5. Return false if no interval matches.

load() fetches the quiet_hours JSONB column from user_preferences for the authenticated user. save() upserts (or updates) the row. Both must handle the case where no row exists yet (insert on first save).
acceptance_tests:
Given QuietHoursService.shared.entries is []
When isQuietNow() is called at any time
Then it returns false (AT-1 default state)

Given an entry {day:"mon", start:"22:00", end:"07:00"} (midnight-spanning)
When isQuietNow() is called at 23:30 on Monday
Then it returns true (AT-5)

Given the same midnight-spanning entry
When isQuietNow() is called at 06:00 on Monday
Then it returns true (AT-5)

Given two entries for the same day {start:"12:00",end:"13:00"} and {start:"22:00",end:"07:00"}
When isQuietNow() is checked against each interval
Then both intervals are respected independently (AT-10)

Given an entry {start:"00:00", end:"23:59"} for a day
When isQuietNow() is called at any time on that day
Then it returns true (AT-8)

Given the user saves new entries via save()
When the app checks isQuietNow() immediately after
Then the new entries are used without app restart (AT-6)

title: iOS: implement QuietHoursSettingsView (SwiftUI settings UI)
spec:
Create QuietHoursSettingsView.swift in the zazigv3 iOS app. It must:

- Show a master toggle (Quiet Hours on/off). When toggled off, save an empty array [] to Supabase immediately.
- When enabled, display 7 day rows (Mon–Sun), each row having:
  - A per-day toggle (enabled/disabled)
  - Start time picker (DatePicker in .hourAndMinute mode, 24h)
  - End time picker (DatePicker in .hourAndMinute mode, 24h)
- Preset buttons:
  - "Weeknights": populates Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri with start=22:00, end=07:00 entries
  - "Weekends": populates Sat, Sun with start=00:00, end=23:59 entries
- Auto-save via Combine: debounce changes 500ms, then call QuietHoursService.shared.save().
- Call QuietHoursService.shared.load() on .onAppear.
- @ObservedObject / @StateObject from QuietHoursService.shared for reactive updates.
acceptance_tests:
Given the user opens QuietHoursSettingsView with no saved entries
When the view appears
Then the master toggle is off and no time pickers are visible (AT-1)

Given the user enables quiet hours and sets Monday 09:00–17:00
When 500ms elapses after the last change
Then the entry is persisted to Supabase (AT-2)

Given the user taps the master toggle off
When the save debounce fires
Then entries is cleared to [] and Supabase is updated with [] (AT-7)

Given the user taps the "Weeknights" preset button
When the preset is applied
Then Mon–Fri entries each have start=22:00 and end=07:00 (AT-11)

Given the user taps the "Weekends" preset button
When the preset is applied
Then Sat and Sun entries each have start=00:00 and end=23:59 (AT-12)

title: iOS: add QuietHoursSettingsView to Settings navigation stack
spec: Wire QuietHoursSettingsView into the existing iOS Settings navigation stack in zazigv3. Locate the existing Settings screen/coordinator that lists user-facing settings rows. Add a NavigationLink (or equivalent route entry) that navigates to QuietHoursSettingsView. The row label should read "Quiet Hours". Ensure the route is accessible at the top level of the Settings navigation stack — not nested inside an unrelated section.
acceptance_tests:
Given the user opens the app Settings screen
When they scroll through settings rows
Then they see a "Quiet Hours" row (AT-1 — accessible)

Given the user taps the "Quiet Hours" row
When navigation occurs
Then QuietHoursSettingsView is presented (AT-6 — reachable without restart)

title: iOS: add isQuietNow() guard to Realtime pipeline notification dispatch
spec:
In the zazigv3 iOS codebase, locate the Realtime pipeline event handler that dispatches push notifications (wherever UNUserNotificationCenter.add() or equivalent notification scheduling is called for pipeline events). Add the following guard before the notification dispatch:

```swift
guard !QuietHoursService.shared.isQuietNow() else { return }
```

This silently drops the notification surfacing if the current time falls within a quiet window. The Realtime subscription itself must remain active — do not disconnect or pause the subscription. Only the notification display is suppressed.
acceptance_tests:
Given the user has a quiet hours entry covering the current time and day
When a pipeline Realtime event fires
Then no push notification is shown to the user (AT-3)

Given the current time is outside all configured quiet windows
When a pipeline Realtime event fires
Then the push notification surfaces normally (AT-4)

Given quiet hours is disabled (entries = [])
When a pipeline event fires at any time
Then notifications always surface (AT-4, AT-7)

Given a midnight-spanning quiet window (22:00–07:00) on the current day
When a pipeline event fires at 23:30
Then no notification is shown (AT-5)

Given the same midnight-spanning window
When a pipeline event fires at 06:00
Then no notification is shown (AT-5)

title: iOS: mirror quiet hours to App Group UserDefaults for Notification Service Extension
spec:
If a UNNotificationServiceExtension target exists in the zazigv3 project, add quiet hours suppression to it. Since extensions cannot access Supabase directly:

1. In QuietHoursService.save(), after saving to Supabase, also write the serialized entries array to a shared App Group UserDefaults key (e.g., "quiet_hours_entries") using the App Group identifier already configured for the project.
2. In QuietHoursService.load(), after loading from Supabase, also sync the App Group UserDefaults with the fetched entries.
3. In the Notification Service Extension's didReceive(_:withContentHandler:), read the entries from App Group UserDefaults and apply the same isQuietNow() logic (duplicate or share the algorithm). If quiet now, call contentHandler with empty/nil content or suppress the notification per UNNotificationServiceExtension conventions.

If no UNNotificationServiceExtension target exists in the project, document this clearly in a code comment within QuietHoursService.swift and skip this job's implementation — mark the job done with a note.
acceptance_tests:
Given a UNNotificationServiceExtension exists and quiet hours covers the current time
When a push arrives and the extension's didReceive fires
Then the notification is suppressed (AT-3)

Given the user updates quiet hours in the app
When save() completes
Then App Group UserDefaults reflects the new entries so the extension uses them immediately (AT-6)

Given no UNNotificationServiceExtension exists in the project
When this job is executed
Then a comment is added to QuietHoursService.swift noting the extension integration is a no-op (graceful skip)
